source_citation: Baerson et al. 2002 Plant Physiology 129:1265-1275; PMID 12114580
notes: Peer-reviewed GenBank-linked accession pair: PubMed metadata lists AJ417033/AJ417034; GenBank records label AJ417033 as epsps-R and AJ417034 as epsps-S. Baerson reports mature-protein Pro106Ser; GenBank translated partial-protein difference is position 107 (resistant=Ser, susceptible=Pro), mapped by local alignment to 8UMJ maize sequence position 107 / PDB residue 106. Correction note: 8UMJ includes an initiating MET numbered 0, so FASTA sequence position 107 corresponds to PDB residue 106, not 108. AJ417033 also carries a second translated difference at position 382 (resistant=Leu, susceptible=Pro), but Baerson reports that the second substitution does not contribute significantly to reduced glyphosate sensitivity.
